messages.content: edit this part of article to have a correct grammar and remove extra preposition and fix this for ISI journal "Choosing and purchasing air conditioning equipment for large office or educational buildings can be a complicated process due to the high investment cost, regardless of the location of the building or the type of equipment available, another critical factor to consider in the selection process is to ensure that the equipment selected can maintain the building's cooling load requirements over the long term. Among the various factors that add to the challenge of accurately predicting cooling loads, one that is becoming increasingly problematic is global warming."

Choosing and purchasing air conditioning equipment for large office or educational buildings can be a complicated process due to the high investment cost, regardless of the building's location or the availability of equipment. Another critical factor to consider in the selection process is ensuring that the chosen equipment can adequately meet the building's cooling load requirements in the long term. Among the various challenges that contribute to accurately predicting cooling loads, one that is increasingly problematic is global warming.

edit this part of article to have a correct grammar and remove extra preposition and fix this for ISI journal "In today’s era of rising temperatures and climate change, the effects of global warming have become a growing concern for builders, building managers, and HVAC professionals alike. The impact of global warming has led to a shift in temperature, resulting in altered climate patterns and a subsequent increase in the cooling load requirements of buildings. In this research, the effects of rising temperatures on the required cooling load in the 4 hottest months of the year (June to September) explored."

In today's era of rising temperatures and climate change, the effects of global warming have become a growing concern for builders, building managers, and HVAC professionals in the ISI journal. The impact of global warming has led to temperature shifts, resulting in altered climate patterns and an increase in the cooling load requirements of buildings. This research explores the effects of rising temperatures on the required cooling load during the four hottest months of the year (June to September).

edit this part of article to have a correct grammar and remove extra preposition and fix this for ISI journal "Through the use of the LSTM neural network, the input parameters for the MLP neural network predicted, which helped to calculate the cooling load for the building from 2023 to 2060. Figure 9 shows the Global temperature anomalies, according to this reference, the world temperature will increase about 1 °C until 2060."

Using the LSTM neural network, the predicted input parameters for the MLP neural network aided in calculating the building's cooling load from 2023 to 2060 for the ISI journal. Figure 9 illustrates global temperature anomalies; according to this reference, world temperatures are projected to increase by approximately 1 °C until 2060.

edit this part of article to have a correct grammar and remove extra preposition and fix this for ISI journal "These trends were based on historical data of maximum dry temperature, wet temperature and wind speed, and included actual data from 1951 to 2022 and forecast data from 2023 to 2060 for 4 months of the year. (from June to September)"

These trends were based on historical data of maximum dry temperature, wet temperature, and wind speed, which included actual data from 1951 to 2022 and forecast data from 2023 to 2060 for four months of the year (from June to September). The findings are relevant for the ISI journal.

edit this part of article to have a correct grammar and remove extra preposition and fix this for ISI journal "Figure 10 shows the maximum temperature during July, which is known as the hottest month of the year in Iran, from 1951 to 2060. The LSTM neural network forecast results for the aforementioned four months show a possible increase in maximum temperature of approximately 6°C by 2060. Figure 11 shows the average annual wet temperature and wind speed results obtained by averaging the values of these parameters in a 24-hour period every day in the specified period of these four months. Subsequently, based on the employment of the aforementioned trio of parameters and establishment of an independent (MLP) network to discern relative humidity, the final time-dependent parameter can be prognosticated on an hourly basis until the year 2060. Moreover, employing the entirety of input parameters within the MLP neural network, the cooling load value is similarly projected on an hourly basis within the specified four-month interval until 2060. "

Figure 10 illustrates the maximum temperature during July, known as the hottest month of the year in Iran, from 1951 to 2060. The LSTM neural network's forecast results for these four months indicate a potential increase in the maximum temperature of approximately 6°C by 2060. Figure 11 displays the average annual wet temperature and wind speed results, obtained by averaging the values of these parameters over a 24-hour period each day within the specified four-month period. Furthermore, by employing the trio of parameters mentioned above and implementing an independent MLP neural network to determine relative humidity, the final time-dependent parameter can be predicted on an hourly basis until the year 2060. Additionally, by utilizing all input parameters in the MLP neural network, the cooling load value can be similarly projected on an hourly basis within the specified four-month duration until 2060. These findings are relevant for the ISI journal.

edit this part of article to have a correct grammar and remove extra preposition and fix this for ISI journal "Figure 12 shows the impact of global warming on the city of Mashhad, particularly explaining that a 6°F increase in atmospheric temperature would result in a significant 26% increase in total cooling demand over a four-month time frame in 2060, in Compared to the demand recorded in 2022. Note that the results shown in this graph are based on the selection of the interior design temperature of 75 °F."

Figure 12 demonstrates the impact of global warming on the city of Mashhad. Specifically, it highlights that a 6°F increase in atmospheric temperature would lead to a significant 26% rise in total cooling demand over a four-month period in 2060, when compared to the demand recorded in 2022. It is important to note that these results are based on a selected interior design temperature of 75°F. These findings are relevant for the ISI journal.

edit this part of article to have a correct grammar and remove extra preposition and fix this for ISI journal "When it comes to designing a building's interior plan, one of the most important considerations is how to maintain comfortable temperatures throughout the year. This involves calculating the cooling and heating loads required to keep the indoor environment at a desirable temperature range. However, the temperature range for calculating these loads can vary depending on the climate zone and local weather conditions."

When it comes to designing the interior plan of a building, one of the utmost important considerations is maintaining comfortable temperatures year-round. This entails calculating the necessary cooling and heating loads to ensure the indoor environment remains within a desirable temperature range. However, it's crucial to note that the temperature range for calculating these loads may vary depending on the climate zone and local weather conditions. These factors are of significance for the ISI journal.

edit this part of article to have a correct grammar and remove extra preposition and fix this for ISI journal "In warmer climates, the cooling load is typically calculated based on maintaining a temperature range of 75-78°F (24-26°C) indoors. This range is also considered comfortable for most people and helps to prevent discomfort or health issues caused by exposure to excessive heat. Factors such as solar gain, ventilation, and humidity levels are taken into account when calculating the cooling load, as they can significantly affect the amount of energy required to maintain this temperature range."

In warmer climates, the calculation of the cooling load typically involves maintaining an indoor temperature range of 75-78°F (24-26°C). This range is widely recognized as comfortable for the majority of individuals and aids in preventing discomfort and health problems associated with excessive heat exposure. When determining the cooling load, various factors such as solar gain, ventilation, and humidity levels are carefully considered as they can have a significant impact on the energy needed to uphold this temperature range. These details are relevant for the ISI journal.

edit this part of article to have a correct grammar and remove extra preposition and fix this for ISI journal "The present study examined the weather conditions of Mashhad city, which are classified as warm climate, in order to determine an appropriate internal design temperature. Specifically, a temperature range of 75-78 °F was evaluated. The results of this investigation are presented in Figure 13, which displays the cooling load at various internal design temperatures across the four summer months. Notably, the results indicate that even a small change of 1 °F can result in a significant alteration of the cooling load for each hour. Consequently, such findings have important implications for cost-saving measures in the long term."

The present study focuses on the weather conditions of Mashhad city, classified as a warm climate, aiming to determine the suitable internal design temperature for optimal comfort. More specifically, a temperature range of 75-78 °F was evaluated. The findings of this investigation, presented in Figure 13, illustrate the cooling load at different internal design temperatures throughout the four summer months. Remarkably, the results indicate that even a slight change of 1 °F can lead to a substantial variation in the cooling load on an hourly basis. Consequently, these findings hold significant implications for long-term cost-saving measures. Such findings are pertinent to the ISI journal.

edit this part of article to have a correct grammar and remove extra preposition and fix this for ISI journal "based on the hourly analysis of the cooling load at varying interior design temperatures, the peak cooling load typically manifests at 16:00 on a majority of days. This phenomenon can be attributed to a confluence of factors, including heightened occupancy levels within the university premises, augmented utilization of electronic devices such as computers and lights, and a rise in outdoor temperature levels. Supporting this finding, Figure 14 presents a breakdown of the cooling load across each hour of July, which is typically considered to be the hottest month of the year, at a design temperature of 75 °F."

Based on hourly analysis of the cooling load at different interior design temperatures, it is observed that the peak cooling load generally occurs at 16:00 on most days. This occurrence can be attributed to a combination of factors such as increased occupancy within the university premises, higher usage of electronic devices like computers and lights, and elevated outdoor temperature levels. Supporting this finding, Figure 14 provides a detailed breakdown of the cooling load for each hour in July, which is widely recognized as the hottest month of the year, at a design temperature of 75 °F. These results are relevant for the ISI journal.

edit this part of article to have a correct grammar and remove extra preposition and fix this for ISI journal "It is noteworthy that the indoor design temperature ranges presented in this study are not definitive and may be subject to variation based on individual preferences or specific building requirements. For instance, certain buildings may necessitate higher or lower temperature ranges depending on their intended use or occupancy. As such, the neural network model was developed within the temperature range of 72-78 °F. Figure 15 depicts the cooling load at 16:00 for each day of the year under varying interior design temperatures, while Figure 16 shows the average cooling load per working hour of the building at different interior design temperatures in 2022. The findings indicate that a mere 1°F shift in temperature leads to average 1.51% change in the cooling load during working hours, thereby underscoring the potential for significant energy savings if this scenario is extrapolating to several years to come."

It is important to note that the indoor design temperature ranges presented in this study are not definitive and may undergo variation based on individual preferences or specific building requirements. For instance, certain buildings may necessitate higher or lower temperature ranges depending on their intended use or occupancy. Consequently, the neural network model was developed within the temperature range of 72-78 °F. Figure 15 illustrates the cooling load at 16:00 for each day of the year, considering varying interior design temperatures. Moreover, Figure 16 displays the average cooling load per working hour of the building at different interior design temperatures in 2022. The findings highlight that even a small temperature shift of 1°F results in an average 1.51% change in the cooling load during working hours. This emphasizes the potential for notable energy savings if this scenario is extrapolated over several years to come. These results contribute to the ISI journal.

edit this part of article to have a correct grammar and remove extra preposition and fix this for ISI journal "Figure 17 shows that an increase in the indoor design temperature of 1°F by 5.5% results in a reduction in the average annual cooling load over the next 38 years. As a result, the careful selection of air conditioning system design can save significant energy consumption in the long run and thus curb the environmental risks associated with energy consumption."

Figure 17 demonstrates that a 1°F increase in the indoor design temperature correlates with a 5.5% decrease in the average annual cooling load over the subsequent 38 years. Consequently, meticulous selection of air conditioning system design can lead to substantial energy savings in the long term, thereby mitigating the environmental risks associated with energy consumption. These findings contribute to the ISI journal.

edit this part of article to have a correct grammar and remove extra preposition and fix this for ISI journal "The present study introduces a novel hybrid NN, integrating two MLP and LSTM models, with the objective of examining the impact of global warming on the building’s cooling load demand throughout a duration of approximately 40 years, specifically focusing on the four hottest months. Additionally, the investigation showcases the influence of interior design temperature on cooling load, aiming to identify the optimal performance strategy. The subsequent sections outline the pivotal findings of this investigation."

The present study introduces a novel hybrid neural network (NN) that integrates two models, MLP and LSTM. Its objective is to examine the effect of global warming on the cooling load demand of the building over a span of approximately 40 years, with a specific focus on the four hottest months. Furthermore, the investigation explores the impact of interior design temperature on the cooling load, aiming to identify optimal performance strategies. The ensuing sections elucidate the crucial findings derived from this investigation. These contributions are significant for the ISI journal.

edit this part of article to have a correct grammar and remove extra preposition and fix this for ISI journal  "•	The neural network architecture that exhibited superior accuracy, as quantified by the lowest Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) and Mean Absolute Error (MAE), in forecasting the building’s cooling load encompassed three hidden layers, each comprising 20 neurons.
•	Based on global projections, it is anticipated that the Earth’s mean annual temperature will rise by approximately 1°C by the year 2060. However, the empirical findings presented in this study indicate that, for the city of Mashhad, Iran, during the peak summer period in July, the average air temperature is projected to escalate by approximately 6°C.

•	The rise in temperature by 6 °C witnessed in the four hot months (from June to September) of the year 2060, in comparison to the year 2022, entails a noteworthy augmentation of approximately 26% in the overall cooling load. This analysis factors in the selection of an interior design temperature of 75 °F.

•	Based on an hourly analysis of the cooling load for the subject under study, it is determined that the building's maximum cooling load demand is at 16:00, as influenced by the prevailing weather conditions in Mashhad, the duration of sunshine, and the building materials employed.

•	The conducted research on internal design temperature reveals that a slight alteration of 1°F in temperature results in an average modification of 1.51% in the cooling load experienced during operational hours. This finding emphasizes the considerable scope for achieving noteworthy energy savings if such a trend extends over subsequent years.

•	The augmentation of indoor design temperature by 1°F leads to a decrease of approximately 5.5% in the average annual cooling load for the subsequent 38-year period.
"

• The neural network architecture that demonstrated superior accuracy, measured by the lowest Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) and Mean Absolute Error (MAE), in forecasting the building's cooling load contained three hidden layers, each with 20 neurons.

• Global projections indicate an expected increase in the Earth's mean annual temperature of approximately 1°C by the year 2060. However, the empirical findings presented in this study indicate that during the peak summer period in Mashhad, Iran, specifically in July, the average air temperature is projected to rise by approximately 6°C.

• The projected 6°C temperature increase in the four hot months (June to September) in 2060, compared to 2022, results in a significant 26% rise in the overall cooling load. This analysis considers an interior design temperature of 75°F.

• Analyzing the cooling load on an hourly basis reveals that the maximum cooling load demand for the building occurs at 16:00, influenced by Mashhad's prevailing weather conditions, duration of sunshine, and building materials utilized.

• The research conducted on interior design temperature indicates that a mere 1°F temperature change leads to an average 1.51% modification in the cooling load during operational hours. This finding highlights the potential for substantial energy savings if such a trend continues in the following years.

• Increasing the indoor design temperature by 1°F results in an approximate 5.5% reduction in the average annual cooling load over the subsequent 38-year period.

edit this part of article to have a correct grammar and remove extra preposition and fix this for ISI journal "The acquired findings offer guidance in selecting the most suitable performance strategy during the four hottest months of the year, taking into account local conditions influenced by increasing of global warming."

The findings obtained from this study provide informative guidance for selecting the most appropriate performance strategy during the four hottest months of the year. These recommendations take into account local conditions influenced by the increasing effects of global warming. These insights contribute to the ISI journal.

edit this part of article to have a correct grammar and remove extra preposition and fix this for ISI journal "To extend the scope of this plan, it is possible to apply this strategy throughout the entire year to assess the heating load of the building and determine the most optimal performance strategy through conducting economic and energy analyses."

To broaden the scope of this approach, it is plausible to incorporate this strategy for year-round application, assessing both the cooling and heating load of the building. This expanded analysis would enable the identification of the most optimal performance strategy. Additionally, conducting economic and energy analyses would further enhance the evaluation process within the context of the ISI journal.
